name: Chilled Cucumber Soup (Weir)
recipeIngredient: 1 large English cucumber, peeled and halved
6 paper-thin cucumber slices with skin (for garnish)
3 tablespoons white wine vinegar or freshly squeezed lemon juice
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a sieve with cheesecloth (muslin) and place it over a large bowl.
2. Spoon the yogurt into the sieve and let drain in the refrigerator for 4 hours.
3. Discard the captured liquid and transfer the yogurt to the bowl.
4. Add the grated cucumber, garlic, olive oil, mint, chopped dill, and milk.
5. Mix well. Stir in the vinegar or lemon juice.
6. Cover and chill for 1 hour.
7. Before serving, season to taste with salt and pepper.
8. Ladle the soup into chilled individual bowls and garnish each serving with a cucumber slice and a dill sprig.
9. Serve well chilled. Serves 6
NOTES: Cucumbers are native to Asia, where they have been eaten for thousands of years. They contain a great deal of water, making them particularly refreshing in the hot summer months. When choosing cucumbers, avoid waxed varieties sold year-round and instead seek out long, thin-skinned English or hothouse varieties, which have superior flavor, less water, and far fewer seeds. Three lemon cucumbers can be substituted for a single English cucumber if desired.
